congruence_10.label: Congruence & Triangles
misconceptions: Congruent triangles must have the same orientation
CPCTC can be applied before proving congruence
congruence_10.advanced: Proves triangle congruence using all postulates and applies CPCTC to prove additional relationships. Constructs congruent triangles with compass and straightedge. Proves and applies properties of isosceles and equilateral triangles. Uses congruence to solve complex geometric problems.
congruence_10.proficient: Identifies congruence postulates (SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS) and proves triangles congruent. Applies CPCTC to find missing parts. Uses congruence relationships in proofs and to solve problems.
congruence_10.developing: Identifies congruent triangles when given side/angle measures. Applies SSS and SAS postulates with support. Struggles to set up or execute formal proofs using CPCTC.
congruence_10.beginning: Recognizes congruent triangles visually. Cannot yet reliably identify which postulate applies or prove congruence formally.

similarity_10.label: Similarity & Right Triangles
misconceptions: Scale factor is applied the same way in one and two dimensions
Tan(90°) is undefined for a conceptual reason
similarity_10.advanced: Proves similarity using all criteria and applies similarity to solve real-world problems involving scale and proportion. Uses trigonometric ratios in complex contexts including angles of elevation/depression. Applies the Pythagorean theorem in multi-step problems and proves its converse.
similarity_10.proficient: Proves triangles similar using AA, SSS, and SAS similarity. Solves problems using properties of similar figures and scale factors. Correctly applies trigonometric ratios to find missing sides and angles in right triangles.
similarity_10.developing: Identifies similar triangles and uses scale factors to find missing lengths. Applies trigonometric ratios with support to find sides in right triangles. Uses Pythagorean theorem correctly.
similarity_10.beginning: Recognizes similar figures but struggles to prove similarity. Cannot reliably apply scale factors or use trigonometric ratios. Needs significant support with Pythagorean theorem applications.

circle_10.label: Circles & Arc Measures
misconceptions: Central and inscribed angles subtending the same arc have equal measures
A tangent line can intersect a circle at two points
Arc length and arc measure are the same
circle_10.advanced: Applies relationships between chords, arcs, and angles in complex configurations. Proves tangent-radius perpendicularity and solves problems involving tangent segments. Calculates arc length and sector area. Finds equations of circles and analyzes circles in coordinate geometry.
circle_10.proficient: Identifies relationships between central and inscribed angles. Finds arc measures and applies inscribed angle theorem. Calculates circumference, arc length, and sector area. Writes circle equations in standard form.
circle_10.developing: Finds arc measures for central angles and applies inscribed angle theorem with support. Calculates circumference and basic arc lengths. Struggles with sector area and circle equations.
circle_10.beginning: Recognizes circles and their parts. Understands basic angle-arc relationships. Cannot yet calculate circumference, arc length, or sector area independently.

volume_10.label: Area & Volume of 3D Figures
misconceptions: Volume and surface area use the same formula
All pyramids have the same volume formula constant
Scaling side length by k scales volume by k
volume_10.advanced: Finds volumes and surface areas of complex and composite 3D figures. Applies scaling to understand how volume and surface area change with dimensions. Solves real-world problems involving volume and surface area. Explains relationships between different solids.
volume_10.proficient: Calculates volume and surface area for prisms, pyramids, cylinders, cones, and spheres. Solves problems involving composite figures. Applies formulas correctly and shows understanding of when each formula is used.
volume_10.developing: Calculates volumes for standard solids with a reference formula. Struggles with surface area or composite figures. May confuse which formula to apply.
volume_10.beginning: Recognizes different 3D figures. Knows volume formulas exist but cannot apply them reliably or independently select correct formulas.

coordinate_10.label: Coordinate Geometry Proofs
misconceptions: Slopes of parallel lines are equal only if positive
The distance formula only works for horizontal or vertical segments
Coordinate proofs always use algebraic calculations only
coordinate_10.advanced: Uses coordinate geometry to prove properties of figures algebraically and geometrically. Proves complex properties including relationships between slopes, distances, and midpoints. Applies multiple proof strategies and explains why coordinate proofs are effective.
coordinate_10.proficient: Uses distance and midpoint formulas accurately. Determines if lines are parallel or perpendicular using slopes. Proves properties of triangles (isosceles, right) and quadrilaterals (parallelogram, rectangle) using coordinates.
coordinate_10.developing: Applies distance and midpoint formulas with support. Finds slopes and determines basic parallel/perpendicular relationships. Attempts coordinate proofs but may have calculation errors.
coordinate_10.beginning: Recognizes that coordinates can be used to verify geometric properties. Cannot yet reliably apply formulas or construct proofs independently.
